name: Alaior
highlights: The white old town set upon the hill
Artisan cheese and ice cream (DOP Mahón-Menorca)
The Torre d'en Galmés site close by
blurb: A whitewashed inland town, home of Mahón-Menorca DOP cheese and of artisan ice cream and cured meats. Founded by Jaume II around the hill of Ihalor…

name: Licence-free boat hire from Ciutadella
highlights: sail without a licence (boats for up to 5 people, 15 hp engine) up to 5 miles out
drop anchor and swim freely in the coves and waters of the west
hire by the half-day or full day from the harbour of Ciutadella
blurb: Skipper your own boat without a licence from the harbour of Ciutadella and discover the western coves at your own pace. Boats for up to five people…

name: Traditional Llaüt Hire / Private Sunset Boat
highlights: sail the south-west's unspoilt coves by sea, away from the crowds
anchor off Macarella or Turqueta and swim straight from the boat
sunset on board with cava; book a skipper in advance in summer
blurb: A trip aboard a wooden Menorcan llaüt — the island's elegant traditional craft, with a sun deck — or a private skippered boat from Cala Galdana or…

name: Aquarock (water park, Cala'n Bosch)
highlights: swim in the only wave pool in Menorca
slides for older children and a safe splash zone for the little ones
a full day with a restaurant, sun loungers and go-karting; tickets cheaper online
blurb: The big water park of the west, next to Cala'n Bosch: the island's only wave pool, slides for every age and a children's area for the youngest. The…

name: Artrutx Sea Club
blurb: A sea club beside the Cap d'Artrutx lighthouse: restaurant and cocktail bar facing the sea, with one of the west's finest sunsets.
shuttleInfo: Drive from Ciutadella (~8-10 km south, 15-20 min). Restaurant's private car park and roadside parking (fills up early in summer: arrive ahead of time).
effortNote: Flat terrace next to the Cap d'Artrutx lighthouse.

name: Sunset by boat from Binibeca
highlights: sailing the south-eastern coast of whitewashed cottages as evening falls
dropping anchor in a sheltered cove for a swim
a toast with cava and snacks on board
blurb: A sunset boat trip from Cala Torret, next to Binibeca, with cava and cold drinks on board and the whitewashed fishermen's cottages set against the…

name: Sunset boat trip across the harbour of Maó
highlights: taking in the great harbour of Maó in the warm light of dusk
a toast with cava and cold drinks on board
a central departure from Cala Figuera, with no need for a car
blurb: A private sunset trip across the natural harbour of Maó, one of the largest in the Mediterranean, with a glass of cava and the city lighting up in…

name: Sunset boat trip from Ciutadella
highlights: boarding in the historic harbour of Ciutadella, without a car
watching the sun fall over the sea on the western coast
the golden hour with the light low over the water
blurb: A boat trip from the harbour of Ciutadella, on the western coast, to watch the sun set from the water — without a car and at a serene pace.

name: Sunset sail by catamaran from Fornells
highlights: sailing through the northern marine reserve at sunset
dropping anchor in a sheltered cove for a last swim
a toast with drinks and snacks as the sun goes down
blurb: A catamaran sail from the harbour of Fornells to watch the sun set behind Cap de Cavalleria, with a quiet anchorage for a swim and drinks on board…

name: Binibeca Vell
blurb: A whitewashed village (1972) imitating a fishing hamlet, with maze-like lanes and postcard corners. It is private property.
seasonalHours: Private property with restricted visiting hours; respect the quiet and the access rules. Avoid the midday rush.

name: Binisafua
highlights: bathing in a quiet residential cove, away from the bustle
seeing the traditional boat houses up close
gentle snorkelling among the rocks (bring water shoes)
blurb: A small, quiet cove with a residential feel, marked by boathouses and rock rather than a broad expanse of sand — more a spot for a local dip. Well…

name: Birdwatching at s'Albufera des Grau (park trails)
highlights: walk the Santa Madrona trail (2.8 km, the best for water birds) with its hides over the lagoon
complete the Sa Gola (1.7 km, dunes and beach) and Cala Llimpa (1.7 km, Ses Puntes viewpoint) trails
start at the Centre de Recepció Rodríguez Femenias (Maó–Es Grau road, km 3.5) to get your bearings
blurb: The only nature park in Menorca and the heart of the Biosphere Reserve: three short, level trails with hides over the lagoon to watch herons…

name: Bodega Binitord
highlights: walk the vineyard and learn how the wine is made with the team themselves
taste their wines (white, rosé, red) and the first vermouth made in Menorca
book the guided visit online (ES/EN/FR), around 1 hr
blurb: A family winery built over a former quarry south of Ciutadella, with 7 ha of vines and around 30,000 bottles a year of Vi de la Terra Illa de Menorca…

name: Sa Bodega de Son Cremat
highlights: walk among the vines and taste their whites and rosé with the owners
experience the harvest in late summer (a seasonal experience)
call ahead: visits by appointment only
blurb: A small family winery in the countryside near Maó, on the road to the airport, which planted its first vines in 2010. Young chardonnay and moscatel…

name: Vinya Sa Cúdia
highlights: discover an unirrigated malvasía vineyard inside the natural park
taste their whites (Favàritx, Sa Cudia) and the Favàritx red
arrange your visit in advance (by appointment only)
blurb: A family winery run by six siblings within the Parc Natural de s'Albufera des Grau, on one of the few slate soils in the Balearics. Unirrigated…

name: Bodegas Binifadet
highlights: the guided visit through the vineyard with a tasting of Menorca's wine
lunch or dinner on the terrace among the vines
booking the tasting and the table in advance
blurb: Menorca's oldest and largest winery (around 100,000 bottles a year, half the island's output, and the only producer of sparkling wines). Twelve…

name: Bodegas Menorquinas (Ferrer de Muntpalau)
highlights: discover the winery that started Menorca's own regional wine
taste their whites, aged reds and the Fusió
arrange your visit by phone before you go
blurb: The pioneering winery of Vi de la Terra Illa de Menorca (the island's regional wine appellation): it began in 1987 under the wing of the historic Ca…

name: Cycle touring and mountain biking on the Camí de Cavalls (Velos Joan hire)
highlights: hire a quality mountain, gravel or e-bike (hotel delivery available)
ride the cyclable sections of the Camí de Cavalls (mountain-bike routes in Basic, Standard or Plus versions)
choose the level to match your experience; the hardest sections are for expert riders only
blurb: Discover the coast on two wheels: parts of the Camí de Cavalls are open to mountain bikes, and the island is made for cycling. Velos Joan, a leading…

name: Diving in the Northern Marine Reserve (Fornells Diving Center / Dune)
highlights: dives in the northern marine reserve with excellent visibility
try-dives for beginners, PADI courses and snorkelling outings
a PADI 5-star centre with its own boats moored opposite the base
blurb: Dive the seabed of the Northern Marine Reserve from Fornells, with exceptional visibility and dive sites fifteen minutes from the bay. A…

name: Diving and try-dives in the Illa de l'Aire reserve (S'Algar Diving)
highlights: a try-dive to get started, or dives in the Illa de l'Aire reserve
more than 20 dive sites and guided snorkelling outings
a PADI 5-star centre with a practice pool, classrooms and its own boats
blurb: Dive into the Illa de l'Aire Marine Reserve with a PADI centre that has spent more than thirty years on the south-east coast. From a beginner's…

name: Cala Algaiarens
highlights: bathing at two wild sister beaches (es Bot and es Tancats)
walking the pine wood and dunes of an undeveloped protected area (ANEI)
arriving early in summer: access and parking are regulated
blurb: Two sister beaches (es Bot and es Tancats) of golden-red sand within an unspoilt protected area framed by pine wood and dunes, free of buildings. One…

name: Cala Binidali
highlights: the swim in very clear turquoise water
snorkelling among the cliffs that enclose it
arriving early: it is small and intimate and fills up
blurb: A small, natural cove of fine golden sand and clear turquoise water, tucked between cliffs and excellent for snorkelling. Intimate, with limited…

name: Cala Blanca
highlights: fine white sand with full services, a short hop from Ciutadella
shallow, calm waters, comfortable for families
direct access by car: a good base to stay near the water
blurb: A built-up cove of some 50 metres of very white, fine sand, with every amenity and close to Ciutadella. Practical for staying and bathing without…

name: Platja de Cavalleria
highlights: the great wild beach of reddish sand beneath ochre cliffs
the easy parking by the cove and the descent on wooden walkways
climbing afterwards to the Cap de Cavalleria lighthouse at sunset
blurb: A large unspoilt beach of reddish sand framed by ochre cliffs, one of the north's icons. Convenient thanks to its car park near the cove.
effortNote: A narrow paved road, ~15 min to the free car park; from there ~400 m on foot down wooden stairs.

name: Cala de Biniancolla
highlights: the calm swim in water that deepens little by little (good for children)
the fishermen's boat houses nearby
rice and fish right by the cove at En Caragol
blurb: A small, traditional cove of around 100 metres between Punta Prima and Binibeca, with fishermen's huts, a gently shelving seabed and rocky cliffs…

name: Cala de Llucalari
highlights: walk the short path from Son Bou to this hidden cove
enjoy a quiet, little-visited cove with no facilities
bring water and shade: there is no beach bar or anything at the cove
blurb: A small, hidden cove near Son Bou, reached by a short walk. Quiet and little frequented thanks to its foot access; no facilities.

name: Cala del Pilar
highlights: one of the wildest, most isolated beaches in the north
the walk through pine wood and scrub to the golden sand beneath red clay
going with nothing and coming back with nothing: not a single facility
blurb: One of Menorca's wildest and most isolated beaches: golden sand beneath red clay cliffs, with not a single facility. The long walk and access…

name: Cala des Talaier
highlights: a secluded, sheltered cove with an islet just off the shore
less crowded as it requires a walk: bring a picnic, water and shade
pale sand and calm waters, ideal to unwind as a couple
blurb: A small, secluded and sheltered cove of pale sand and calm water, with an islet just off the shore. The walk in keeps it less crowded than Son Saura…

name: Cala en Bosch
highlights: a family beach beside the marina and the inner lagoon (El Lago)
fish and rice restaurants around the marina, facing the water
a flat seafront promenade to the Cap d'Artrutx lighthouse and Son Xoriguer
blurb: A mid-sized developed beach beside a marina and an inland lake (El Lago) ringed by restaurants and apartments. Comfortable and well served, geared…

name: Cala en Porter
highlights: swim in a cove of fine sand set between high cliffs
go up to the Cova d'en Xoroi, in the cliff, for a sunset drink
park up in the resort above and walk down to the beach by the steps
blurb: A cove set between high cliffs, with fine sand and calm water, beside a resort with full amenities. Renowned for Cova d'en Xoroi, a clifftop bar…

name: Cala Escorxada
highlights: walk the Camí de Cavalls to an unspoilt 'end of the world' cove
swim in clean waters on a beach that is almost always quiet
go self-sufficient: no facilities, no shade and a long walk
blurb: A remote, unspoilt cove with an end-of-the-world feel, fine sand and clear water, earned after a good walk. For travellers seeking solitude and…

name: Cala Galdana
blurb: A large horseshoe bay sheltered by cliffs, with fine sand, very calm turquoise water and pines to the shore. The base for the southern coves.
effortNote: Large free car park up top; a steep 5-10 min walk down. Developed, with full services.

name: Cala Macarella
highlights: the postcard of the south: a horseshoe of white sand, turquoise and pines
in summer reached by shuttle bus from Ciutadella or on foot along the Camí de Cavalls
no reliable services: head down with a picnic, water and shade
blurb: The definitive postcard of southern Menorca: a horseshoe of white sand, turquoise water and pines down to the shore. Beside it, its little sister…
shuttleInfo: 1 Jun–30 Sep: private cars and motorbikes are banned. Shuttle bus from Ciutadella, licensed taxi, bike or on foot (Camí de Cavalls from Cala Galdana, ~45 min). Off-season: drive to the free car park, 15-20 min walk away.
effortNote: Reached on foot or by shuttle in summer, along the Camí de Cavalls. No reliable services: bring water and shade.

name: Cala Macarelleta
highlights: Macarella's intimate sister, beyond a rocky isthmus
intense turquoise and a secluded feel (partly naturist)
reached on foot from Macarella; no services at all, bring your own
blurb: Macarella's smaller, more intimate sister, separated by a rocky isthmus; intense turquoise water and a partly naturist atmosphere. More secluded and…

name: Cala Mesquida
highlights: the pale-sand beach with calm waters 10 min from Maó
the defence tower above the cove
eating at one of its beachside fish restaurants
blurb: A beach of pale sand and calm water near Maó, with a small cluster of fishermen's houses, restaurants and a defensive tower. Note: it sits on the…

name: Cala Mica
highlights: a shell-shaped wild cove, little known
extending the Camí de Cavalls from Binimel·la or Cavalleria
taking water and shade: no facilities
blurb: A little-known, shell-shaped virgin cove with mixed sand and pebbles, set beside an old farmhouse. It rewards the walker who follows the Camí de…

name: Cala Mitjana
highlights: walk the 1.2 km path from the car park down to the pale-sand cove
watch some leap into the water from the cliffs (with care)
arrive before 10 am in summer: the car park fills up early
blurb: A wide cove of pale sand and clear water, framed by cliffs that some use to leap into the sea. Near Cala Galdana, it pairs beauty with reasonable…
shuttleInfo: Large free dirt car park (~250 spaces) + 1.2 km signposted downhill path. Arrive before 10am in peak season. Bus L52 (Ciutadella) / L51 (Maó); also on foot from Cala Galdana along the Camí de Cavalls.
effortNote: 1.2 km downhill path, mostly easy and family-friendly; not for wheelchairs.

name: Cala Morell
highlights: the cove-harbour among reddish cliffs and white Cycladic houses
visiting the prehistoric necropolis beside the development
the snorkelling in sheltered waters
blurb: A natural harbour-cove tucked between reddish cliffs amid a development of whitewashed, Cycladic-style houses. Beside it lies the prehistoric Cala…

name: Cala Pregonda
highlights: the reddish sand and the islets of one of the north's most iconic coves
walking around 30 min along the Camí de Cavalls from Binimel·la
choosing a day with little north wind; no facilities
blurb: One of the north coast's most iconic coves: reddish sand, islets just offshore and an almost lunar feel. It has no facilities, and that is precisely…
effortNote: No road access to the cove: ~30 min walk (2 km) along the Camí de Cavalls from the Binimel·la car park. No services; sturdy footwear, water and shade are essential.
seasonalHours: June or September; in July/August the Binimel·la car park fills up early. Best with little northerly wind.

name: Cala Presili
highlights: the wild swim at the foot of the Favàritx lighthouse
combining it with Cala Tortuga, its neighbour, on the same day
in summer reached by seasonal bus, not by car
blurb: A small virgin cove next to Cala Tortuga, at the foot of the Favàritx Lighthouse within the natural park. It is usually combined with Tortuga on the…

name: Cala Roja
highlights: a small, secluded red cove, almost always empty
escaping the crowds without straying far from Cavalleria
an intimate corner among the rocks for snorkelling
blurb: The smaller, more intimate sister of Cavalleria: a secluded red cove among the rocks, almost always empty. Ideal for escaping the crowds without…

name: Cala Santandría
highlights: a wind-sheltered cove of white sand and very calm waters
built-up and convenient, with an adapted bathing service in season (May-October)
ideal for families with children, very close to Ciutadella
blurb: A sheltered cove of white sand and calm, clear water, well protected from the wind and ideal for families with young children. Developed and…

name: Cala Tirant
highlights: a broad golden beach between Fornells and Cavalleria
crossing the wooden walkway between its two areas
the more accessible, family-friendly beach option near Fornells
blurb: A broad beach of fine golden sand between Fornells and Cap de Cavalleria, divided into two areas by a hill with a wooden walkway. More accessible and…

name: Cala Tortuga
highlights: the golden sand beneath the lunar slate landscape of Favàritx
the wild setting of the Parc Natural de s'Albufera des Grau
in summer reached by seasonal bus from Maó
blurb: A virgin beach of golden sand beneath the lunar black-slate landscape of Favàritx, within the natural park. Restricted vehicle access and the walk in…

name: Cala Trebalúger
highlights: reach on foot or by boat one of the island's most beautiful unspoilt coves
enjoy white sand, pines down to the shore and a stream to one side
plan your return: a demanding walk and no facilities at all
blurb: One of the most beautiful virgin coves: white sand, limestone cliffs, pines down to the shore and a small stream meeting the sea at one end…

name: Cala en Turqueta
highlights: an almost unreal turquoise and fine sand sheltered by pines
car access still available in summer, but the car park fills early (arrive around 8:00)
a usual starting point to walk to Macarella along the Camí de Cavalls
blurb: Almost unreal turquoise water and fine sand sheltered by pines. More tucked-away and family-friendly, it's one of the best-loved coves in the…
shuttleInfo: Keeps car access in summer, but the car park fills up very early (arrive ~8:00). Seasonal shuttle bus from Ciutadella too. Common starting point for the walk to Macarella.
effortNote: 10-15 min path through pines from the car park; firm but not suitable for wheelchairs or strollers.

name: Cales Coves
highlights: swim in a twin cove ringed by cliffs honeycombed with caves
see from the water the necropolis of prehistoric caves carved into the rock
respect the site: it is a protected archaeological area
blurb: A twin cove enclosed by cliffs honeycombed with a prehistoric necropolis of caves, a Talayotic-Roman site of considerable cultural and scenic value…

name: Easy Camí de Cavalls: Es Grau – Sa Mesquida (family stretch)
highlights: cross the wooden boardwalk over the Sa Mesquida wetland (pushchair-friendly along its level section)
walk the GR-223 through a protected coastal landscape, within the Biosphere Reserve
pair it with a calm swim at the shallow-water beach of Es Grau
blurb: A gentle stretch of the historic Camí de Cavalls (GR-223) between the fishing village of Es Grau and Sa Mesquida beach, deep in the Natural Park. The…

name: Camí de Cavalls · Stage 10: Punta Nati – Ciutadella
highlights: set out from the solitary Punta Nati lighthouse and its dry-stone landscape
see the barraques, the old dry-stone shepherds' constructions
finish with a stroll along the mouth of Ciutadella harbour at sunset
blurb: A short, easy stage (5 km) between the austere Punta Nati lighthouse, in a stony landscape of shepherds' huts, and the harbour of Ciutadella. Perfect…

name: Camí de Cavalls · Stage 13: Cala en Turqueta – Cala Galdana
highlights: walk the Turqueta, Macarella and Macarelleta coves, linked on foot by the Camí de Cavalls
cross the shaded ravine of limestone walls with fig trees and cool greenery
set off at dawn from the Turqueta car park (it fills early in summer) and finish at Cala Galdana
blurb: The most photographed stretch of the historic trail that rings the island: 6.4 km of waymarked path through pine woods and limestone ravines, linking…

name: Camí de Cavalls · Stage 2: Es Grau – Cap de Favàritx
highlights: cross the only nature park in Menorca, the core of the Biosphere Reserve
finish among the black slate rocks and the Favàritx lighthouse
look out over the unspoilt coves of Tortuga and Presili halfway along
blurb: 8.6 km through the heart of the Parc Natural de s'Albufera des Grau, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, out to the lighthouse and the lunar landscape of…

name: Camí de Cavalls · Stage 20: Cala de Sant Esteve – Maó
highlights: start beside Fort Marlborough, an 18th-century British fort at Cala de Sant Esteve
skirt the mouth of the natural harbour of Maó, one of the largest in the Mediterranean
see the imposing Fortalesa de la Mola from the outside
blurb: The stage that completes the loop around the island: 6 easy kilometres steeped in military history beside the great natural harbour of Maó, passing…

name: Camí de Cavalls · Stage 6: Binimel·là – Els Alocs
highlights: reach Cala Pregonda on foot, one of the most iconic sights of the north
walk the unspoilt Tramontana coast, ochre and wind-beaten
go self-sufficient: this is the hardest stage, with no services and no shade
blurb: The wildest and most demanding stretch of the Camí de Cavalls: 8.9 km along the rugged, reddish north coast, with the legendary Cala Pregonda and its…

name: Castell de Sant Felip
highlights: descend into the underground galleries and countermines with a specialist guide
understand the siege of 1782 and the life of the garrison that defended the harbour
complete the military ensemble at the harbour mouth alongside Fort Marlborough and La Mola
blurb: The great fortress that guarded the mouth of Maó harbour from the 16th c., with kilometres of underground tunnels now hidden below ground. It can…

name: Castell de Sant Nicolau
highlights: climb to the terrace of the 17th-century defence tower (marés sandstone)
the sunset over the harbour mouth and the city
the monument to Admiral David G. Farragut, of Ciutadella roots
blurb: A 17th-century defensive tower at the mouth of Ciutadella harbour, beside a viewpoint with a monument to David Glasgow Farragut. It offers sunsets…

name: Catedral de Santa Maria de Ciutadella
highlights: admire the Catalan Gothic nave and the Neoclassical portal
place the city's history: a cathedral over the Andalusi main mosque
link it with a walk through the old town, the Born and the palaces of Ciutadella
blurb: The Gothic cathedral of Menorca, raised after the conquest by Alfonso III (1287) over the former main mosque. The monumental heart of Ciutadella's…

name: Ciutadella de Menorca
blurb: The island's grand former capital: its most monumental old town, with a Gothic cathedral, palaces and a harbour that comes alive at dusk.
effortNote: Flat, pedestrian old town; park outside the centre (regulated zones and perimeter car parks).

name: Cova d'en Xoroi
blurb: A natural cave cut into the cliff at Cala'n Porter: a viewpoint and lounge at sunset, and a club on summer nights. Menorca's most iconic venue.
effortNote: You climb down to the cliff on carved steps; not suitable for wheelchairs or very limited mobility.
seasonalHours: Jun-Aug from 11:30 until dusk; May/Oct from 15:00. Daytime viewpoint (~€8.5), afternoon lounge (~€12), nightclub after dark. Online booking recommended in summer.

name: Cova des Coloms ('the Cathedral') via the Barranc de Binigaus
highlights: walk the leafy Barranc de Binigaus from Es Migjorn Gran, with almost no climb
enter through a discreet opening into a great chamber with a soaring ceiling (a possible prehistoric sanctuary)
extend the walk to the unspoilt beach of Binigaus
blurb: One of the most impressive caves in Menorca, nicknamed 'the Cathedral' for its temple-like proportions (a 50 m central chamber, 24 m high), at the…

name: Gin Xoriguer Distillery
highlights: the shop beside Maó harbour
tasting Menorca's gin
trying pomada (gin with lemonade)
blurb: A historic distillery on Maó harbour that produces Menorca's gin in copper stills more than 250 years old, over a local wood fire, using wine…

name: Eolo Sunset Bar
highlights: a drink as the sun goes down over the cliffs of Cala'n Forcat
one of the finest sunsets on the western coast
a relaxed, stylish beach-bar mood close to Ciutadella
blurb: A stylish beach bar perched on the cliffs of Cala'n Forcat, on the west coast near Ciutadella. Relaxed in mood, with good cocktails and one of the…

name: Es Caló Blanc
highlights: looking out over one of the tiniest coves on the island
a quick dip among the rocks in a curious little spot
combining it with Punta Prima or Binibeca, which are right next door
blurb: A tiny cove, barely a few metres wide, tucked between the rocks and counted among the smallest on the island; a curious, little-known spot for a…

name: Es Canutells
highlights: the sheltered swim in a cove with a local feel
the anchorage of boats and traditional boat houses
quiet snorkelling in calm, little-frequented water
blurb: A small sheltered cove between Cales Coves and Binidalí, with calm water, frequented more by locals than by visitors. A quiet spot with a local feel.

name: Es Castell
highlights: Cales Fonts harbour at sunset
La Mola Fortress
dining on fish at a terrace above the water
blurb: A town of British military design, founded in 1771 as Georgetown beside the mouth of Maó harbour. Its Esplanada square has a town hall with a red…

name: Es Grau
blurb: A golden-sand beach with shallow, calm water beside the fishing village of Es Grau, gateway to the s'Albufera des Grau Natural Park.
effortNote: Accessible beach (boardwalks, amphibious chair, adapted toilet; 1 May–31 Oct), shallow water ideal for children. Pairs with the park trail.

name: Es Mercadal
highlights: climbing Monte Toro at sunset
the quiet inland-town centre
a nearby DOP cheese dairy (Subaida)
blurb: A whitewashed town at the geographic heart of Menorca, at the foot of Monte Toro (the island's highest point, 358 m). Its name comes from the market…

name: Es Migjorn Gran
highlights: The most serene white village in the south
The Binigaus ravine leading to the Cova des Coloms
The walk down to Binigaus beach
blurb: The island's smallest, quietest municipality, where time seems to stand still: detached white houses and the essence of old Menorca. A young town as…

name: Boat trip to the unspoilt southern coves (Menorca Blava, Ciutadella)
highlights: sail the unspoilt south-west: Son Saura, Es Talaier, Turqueta, Macarella and Macarelleta
two swimming stops with direct access from the boat's platform
mixed paella on board and sea caves with no land access; around 7 h
blurb: A day at sea from the old harbour of Ciutadella to the most sought-after coves of the south-west, the ones with no road. A swimming platform, a sun…

name: Llaüt cruise along the north coast (Mar en Calma, Fornells)
highlights: set out from Fornells aboard a traditional Menorcan llaüt (Portobello / Don Pancho)
two stops to swim, snorkel and paddleboard in coves along the north coast
a Menorcan aperitif on board; around 3 h 30 min of calm sailing
blurb: Sail the north coast aboard a wooden llaüt, the island's traditional Menorcan boat, with two swimming stops in crystal-clear water and a Menorcan…

name: Llaüt cruise to the southern coves (Mar en Calma, Cala Galdana)
highlights: sail the south coast between limestone cliffs and pine woods from Cala Galdana
two stops to swim, snorkel and paddleboard off unspoilt coves
a Menorcan aperitif on board; a traditional wooden boat
blurb: Discover the unspoilt southern coves from the water, setting out from Cala Galdana aboard a llaüt, the traditional Menorcan boat, with swimming stops…

name: Cap de Cavalleria
blurb: The island's northernmost cape and lighthouse: dramatic cliffs, wind and immense views. A must at sunset.
effortNote: A narrow but paved road with parking by the lighthouse; barely any walking. Busy at sunset in summer.

name: Punta Nati Lighthouse
highlights: the stony, arid landscape of the north-west, with dry-stone huts (ponts de bens)
the solitary lighthouse 6 km from Ciutadella, on the cliff edge
one of the island's finest sunsets, away from the crowds
blurb: A lighthouse set in the stony, arid landscape of the north-west, dotted with dry-stone shelters (ponts). The solitary, rugged setting is ideal for…

name: Faro de Favàritx
blurb: A lighthouse on a cape of striated black slate that looks like another planet, at the northern edge of the s'Albufera des Grau Natural Park.
shuttleInfo: Car access restricted in summer (approx. 1 Jun–mid Oct): seasonal bus from Maó (line ~43, ~€3.50/trip). Confirm the year's calendar.

name: Ferreries
highlights: The highest village on the island
The artisan market (in season)
The gateway to Cala Galdana
blurb: A rural white village, the highest in Menorca, with a deep-rooted tradition of footwear (including Pretty Ballerinas) and furniture making…

name: Finca Binillubet
highlights: do the artisan cheese workshop with the farmers themselves
the sensory tasting of PDO Mahón-Menorca cheese and local products

blurb: A visit to a working livestock farm with an artisan cheese-making workshop and a sensory tasting of local produce, led by the owners themselves. A…

name: Fornells
blurb: A northern fishing village of white houses above a nearly 3 km natural bay dotted with llaüts. The home of lobster stew and a watersports base.
effortNote: Small pedestrian village; park at the entrance. Limited bus service (better by car).

name: Fort Marlborough
highlights: descend into the underground galleries with a light and sound staging
follow the rock-cut moat up to the high point above the cove
take in the views of the mouth of Maó harbour and the Castell de Sant Felip opposite
blurb: A British fort (1710-1726) carved into the rock above the idyllic Cala de Sant Esteve, facing the Castell de Sant Felip. A theatricalised guided tour…

name: Fortaleza de La Mola
highlights: The 19th-century fortress above the harbour mouth
The tunnels and gun batteries
The views over Maó from the viewpoints
blurb: An imposing military fortress (1848-1875) guarding the entrance to Maó's harbour, with batteries, tunnels, Vickers guns and sea views. The vast…

name: Hort de Sant Patrici
highlights: The cheese museum and the gardens
The winery and the tasting
Booking the visit
blurb: An estate in Ferreries bringing together a Mahón-Menorca DOP cheese dairy, an ethnological cheese museum (housed in a former farm building since…

name: Illa del Rei
highlights: the British naval hospital (1711) restored by volunteers
the Hauser & Wirth art gallery and Piet Oudolf's garden (free admission)
crossing on the shuttle boat from Maó harbour
blurb: An islet in Maó harbour home to the former British military hospital (18th century), beautifully restored by volunteers, and a Hauser & Wirth art…

name: Guided kayak to sea caves from Cala Galdana
highlights: paddle on calm water and slip into the sea caves of the unspoilt coast
choose a guided beginner route, suitable for families and first-timers
single or double kayak hire with equipment included; book ahead
blurb: From the sheltered calm of Cala Galdana, a kayak outing uncovers sea caves and coves reachable only from the water. With guided routes designed for…

name: Kayaking and snorkelling in the Northern Marine Reserve (Fornells)
highlights: paddle the sheltered bay of Fornells and the coastline of the marine reserve
slip into coves and caves with no land access and swim straight from the kayak
guided snorkelling with equipment included and a safety briefing beforehand

name: Lago Spa (Lago Resort)
highlights: Thermal circuit: heated pool, jets, jacuzzi, sauna and Turkish bath
Body and facial rituals and treatments, therapeutic massages
A day spa open to outside visitors, by the sea

name: Lithica – Pedreres de s'Hostal

seasonalHours: 9:30-14:30; also afternoons in high season. Better for heat than rain (it is open-air).

name: Llatzeret de Maó
highlights: cross by boat from Cales Fonts to the walled island of the lazaretto
tour the quarantine courtyards, the chapel and the isolation quarters with a guide
read the public-health history of the Mediterranean in one of the largest natural harbours in the world
blurb: A former quarantine lazaretto (18th-19th c.) on its own island within Maó harbour, ringed by walls. It is visited by crossing over by boat from Cales…

name: Lloc de Menorca (zoo and educational farm)

round off the visit with the 'Talaquàtic' water area and the playground (in summer)
have a picnic in the picnic area; buy your ticket online at a discount

name: Llucmaçanes
highlights: a white rural hamlet off the tourist routes
the south-eastern countryside and its traditional farmhouses
a brief, quiet stop on the way between Maó and the south coast
blurb: A rural hamlet near Maó, a small traditional Menorcan settlement of white houses in the south-eastern countryside. Not a destination in itself but an…

name: Maó
blurb: A cosmopolitan capital with a strong 18th-century British imprint, overlooking the Mediterranean's largest natural harbour. Elegant centre and a gin…
effortNote: A walkable, well-connected capital (buses across the island and to the airport). Paid parking in the centre.

name: Marea Beach House – Sunset Bar (Cala Blanca)
highlights: a signature cocktail while you watch the sun set over the sea
an open terrace facing the water on the western coast
the relaxed feel of a beach house near Ciutadella
blurb: A cocktail terrace facing the Mediterranean at Cala Blanca, on the western coast, made for the sunset drink with music in the background…

name: Mel i Reines — beekeeping experience (Finca Lluriach)
highlights: understand the life of the hive in the kitchen of the Lluriach estate
put on the beekeeper's suit and watch the bees at work in the field
taste honey freshly extracted; small groups (max. 8 people)

name: Mercat des Claustre del Carme
blurb: A food market in an 18th-century Carmelite cloister in the heart of Maó: local produce, homemade fare and craft.
seasonalHours: Maó centre, on foot. The adjoining fish market (Mercat des Peix) opens Tue-Sat 07:00-14:00.

name: Mercat des Peix
highlights: grazing on fresh fish and shellfish in tapas form
Maó's old fish market (1927), now reborn
the food evenings with music at the market (Fri-Sun)
blurb: Mahón's former fish market reborn as a food hall with local soul: fresh fish and seafood, tapas, sandwiches, and island beers and wines. A capital…

name: Mercat Municipal de Ciutadella
highlights: buy shellfish at the fishmonger and have it cooked at the counters under the arcades
graze on tapas and breakfast beneath the arches first thing
the 19th-century iron architecture, unique on Menorca (Plaça de la Llibertat)
blurb: Ciutadella's fish and seafood market, with sun-ripened fruit and vegetables from local growers. The living heart of the western city, a stop for…

name: Monte Toro
blurb: Menorca's highest point (358 m), with the sanctuary of the Mare de Déu del Toro and a 360° viewpoint over the whole island, sea to sea.
effortNote: A winding paved road from Es Mercadal (~10 min) to the summit car park.

name: Can Saura. Museu de Ciutadella

name: Museu de Menorca
highlights: walk through the largest archaeological collection on the island (more than 36,000 pieces), from the Talayotic culture to the Islamic period
stroll the Baroque cloister of the former friary of Sant Francesc

name: Museu de Menorca – El Pequeño Museo (Maó)
highlights: play in 'El Pequeño Museo': a mock archaeological dig and history games
explore the collection of prehistoric and Talayotic Menorca (World Heritage)
an indoor plan on foot in the centre of Maó, ideal in poor weather
blurb: The island's museum, set in a former convent in Maó, with a children's space ('El Pequeño Museo', the Little Museum) where you learn through play: a…

name: Museu Diocesà de Menorca

name: Museu Militar de Menorca

name: Naveta des Tudons

a short stop beside the Me-1, ideal on the way between Ciutadella and the centre

name: Cala Morell Necropolis
highlights: the necropolis of 14 caves cut into the rock (hypogea)
the interior details: columns, windows and reliefs

name: Calescoves Necropolis

combine the visit with a swim in the unspoilt cove of Cales Coves
visit with respect and touch nothing: a protected site

name: Parc Natural de s'Albufera des Grau
highlights: the trail through the lagoon and the Mediterranean woodland
the hides for birdwatching
the heart of the Menorca Biosphere Reserve
blurb: The core of Menorca's Biosphere Reserve: a coastal lagoon, wetland, and Mediterranean woodland with viewpoints and birdwatching trails. The serene…

small-group outings, stable and comfortable for all ages

name: Platja de Sant Tomàs

name: Punta Prima

name: Quesería Subaida
highlights: The guided tour of the estate
The Mahón-Menorca DOP cheese tasting
Book ahead before visiting

name: Restaurant Sa Llagosta

name: Restaurante Smoix

name: Sa Torreta

name: Playa de Son Bou
blurb: Menorca's longest beach (nearly 4 km), with fine sand and shallow water, beach bars and an extensive wetland and dune system behind.
effortNote: Accessible beach: boardwalks, amphibious chair, adapted toilet and support staff (1 May–31 Oct). Large car park, fills early in summer.

name: Son Bou Beach Club

book in summer: the Son Bou area fills up early

name: Son Felip — Algaiarens beekeeping visit

book your place online (new dates each month); comfortable footwear and a cap

name: Son Martorellet (the pure Menorcan horse breed)

name: Son Saura del Sur

access on foot from the car park: bring a picnic, water and shade (no services)

name: Son Xoriguer

departure point for boat trips and water sports; direct access by car

name: Spa Faustino Gran

name: Spa Santa Ponsa (Fontenille)

Yoga classes and a spa day pass open to non-guests

name: Talatí de Dalt

name: Torralbenc
highlights: dine on local produce over coals among the vineyards at sunset
taste the estate-agroturismo's own wine
book ahead: limited capacity and much in demand in summer

name: Torre de Fornells

the interpretation centre inside
the views over the bay of Fornells and the open sea
blurb: An octagonal British-Spanish defensive tower (18th century), built in sandstone above the mouth of Fornells bay. It houses an interpretation centre…

name: Torre d'en Galmés

name: Torre Vella – open-air yoga and massage (Fontenille)

name: Wellness Cugó Gran
